content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Both amendments identify real, verified timestamp-consistency bugs. Code inspection confirms: (1) engine.rs resume path uses two separate Utc::now() calls that diverge while downstream lineage checks require exact timestamp equality, and (2) build_reconciled_transition_events explicitly discards the caller-supplied running_started_at when from_status is Paused, despite both finalization and repair callers passing canonical run start times. Both fixes are narrowly scoped correctness improvements.

## Votes

- `fr-17-4fa1d7d0`: Accept
  Verified in source: engine.rs:1621 captures Utc::now() for the run_resumed journal event, then engine.rs:1648 captures a second Utc::now() for sync_milestone_bead_start, separated by journal persistence I/O. Downstream, effective_attempt_started_at (run.rs:435-449) extracts the canonical start from the RunResumed event's timestamp (first call), while lineage_entry_matches_attempt compares against the milestone start sync timestamp (second call). These diverge by the I/O gap duration. Since resume_attempt_has_exact_lineage gates failed-resume repair on exact equality, the mismatch produces false negatives that suppress repair and leave milestones in stale lifecycle states. Capturing a single timestamp and reusing it across all three sites is the correct fix.
- `fr-17-43f9612e`: Accept
  Verified in source: build_reconciled_transition_events (service.rs:826-827) has an explicit branch that returns `now` when from_status == Paused, ignoring the running_started_at parameter. Both callers at service.rs:2111-2120 and service.rs:2239-2248 pass Some(finalized_run.started_at) and Some(repaired_run.started_at) respectively — real canonical timestamps that represent when the run actually resumed. Discarding these in favor of evaluation-time `now` shifts the synthetic paused->running event forward to completion/repair time, undercounting duration_seconds for the resumed segment. Using running_started_at when available preserves event chronology and produces accurate duration metadata.
